---
title: "Guess the Correlation"
format: html
filters:
  - shinylive
---

## Test Your Intuition

Adjust the number of points, guess the correlation (r) of the scatter plot, and click the button to reveal the true answer!

```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| components: [viewer]
#| viewerHeight: 700

from shiny import App, render, ui, reactive
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# 1. UI Definition (Updated to modern Shiny API)
app_ui = ui.page_sidebar(
    ui.sidebar(
        ui.input_slider("n_points", "Number of Points (N)", min=10, max=500, value=100, step=10),
        ui.input_action_button("btn_next", "Generate Next Trial"),
        ui.input_action_button("btn_reveal", "Reveal Correlation"),
        ui.br(), ui.br(),
        ui.output_ui("correlation_ui")
    ),
    ui.output_plot("scatter_plot", width="500px", height="500px")
)

# 2. Server Logic
def server(input, output, session):
    # Reactive values to hold state across button clicks
    current_r = reactive.Value(np.random.uniform(-1, 1))
    is_revealed = reactive.Value(False)

    # React to "Next Trial" button
    @reactive.Effect
    @reactive.event(input.btn_next)
    def _():
        current_r.set(np.random.uniform(-1, 1))
        is_revealed.set(False)

    # React to "Reveal" button
    @reactive.Effect
    @reactive.event(input.btn_reveal)
    def _():
        is_revealed.set(True)

    # Generate and render the plot
    @render.plot
    def scatter_plot():
        n = input.n_points()
        r = current_r.get()
        
        # Generate correlated data using multivariate normal distribution
        mean = [0, 0]
        cov = [[1, r], [r, 1]]
        x, y = np.random.multivariate_normal(mean, cov, n).T
        
        # Plotting with matplotlib
        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6, 6))
        ax.scatter(x, y, alpha=0.6, color="#2c3e50", edgecolors='white', linewidth=0.5)
        ax.set_xlim(-4, 4)
        ax.set_ylim(-4, 4)
        ax.grid(True, linestyle='--', alpha=0.5)
        ax.set_aspect('equal', adjustable='box')
        ax.set_xlabel("X")
        ax.set_ylabel("Y")
        
        return fig

    # Render the text revealing the correlation
    @render.ui
    def correlation_ui():
        if is_revealed.get():
            val = current_r.get()
            return ui.h4(f"True Correlation (r): {val:.3f}")
        else:
            return ui.h4("True Correlation (r): ???")

app = App(app_ui, server)

```
